items.question: What do the x-axis and y-axis of an ROC curve represent?
items.explanation: ROC plots False Positive Rate on the horizontal axis and True Positive Rate (recall) on the vertical axis as threshold varies.

items.question: AUC = 0.5 for a binary classifier most likely means:
items.explanation: AUC 0.5 corresponds to the diagonal ROC — scores do not systematically rank positives above negatives.

items.question: Which sklearn input is correct for roc_auc_score?
items.explanation: ROC needs real-valued scores on held-out data; hard 0/1 predictions collapse the threshold sweep.

items.question: TPR at a threshold equals:
items.explanation: True Positive Rate is recall — fraction of actual positives correctly identified.

items.question: AUC is best described as:
items.explanation: AUC integrates TPR vs FPR across all thresholds — it judges separability, not one cutoff.

items.question: Four positives all score higher than four negatives. The ROC-AUC is:
items.explanation: Perfect ranking — every positive beats every negative — yields AUC = 1.
